subject: Product Management
title: Respect Switching Costs
description: A 60-second lesson on Respect Switching Costs.

hook.text: Google+ was better organized than Facebook. Zune had more features than iPod. Neither won — switching costs are invisible walls.

buildup.text: Switching costs include lost data, broken habits, abandoned social connections, and the time to learn something new.

discovery.text: For users to switch, your product must be not just better — it must be dramatically better, enough to justify the pain of moving.

twist.text: Most teams overestimate their improvement and underestimate switching costs. A 10% improvement won't dislodge an incumbent.

climax.text: Products that win against incumbents offer a 10x improvement — or a fundamentally different experience, not a slightly better one.

punchline.text: Being a little better isn't enough. You need to be 10x better.

quiz.question: Why do slightly better products often fail to displace incumbents?
options: Switching costs are too high — a 10x improvement is needed to justify the pain of moving
